extraction.one_line_summary: GAUDU David won from a reduced breakaway with 4km to go on Alto de la Aitana, while CARAPAZ Richard attacked in the GC group but gained only 24 seconds on ROGLIČ Primož.
tactical_patterns: MOVISTAR planted three riders in 34-rider breakaway then SOLER Marc bridged at kilometer 29 showing preplanned double-agent tactics.
GESINK Robert maintained high tempo before Puerto de las Basas forcing 40kph average for two hours, neutralizing GC attacks early.
CARAPAZ Richard waited for CARTHY Hugh to initiate GC attack before countering, avoiding wind exposure while gaining maximum time.
Jumbo-Visma stopped chasing once MÄDER-DONOVAN-IZAGIRRE break gained 40 seconds, calculating stage loss acceptable to protect 24-second GC lead.

rider_signals.GAUDU David: positive: Second stage win shows cold/rainy conditions advantage, attacked from reduced break with 4km remaining when others hesitated.
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: positive: Strong enough to drop CARTHY Hugh with 3km left, gained time on ROGLIČ but tactical delay cost podium opportunity.
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: negative: Required full team pacing to limit losses to 24 seconds, vulnerable when isolated on final uncategorized climb.
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: neutral: Bridged 29km from summit after teammate acceleration but couldn't contribute to final winning move from break.
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: neutral: Initiated GC attack but lacked acceleration to hold CARAPAZ wheel, still secured first Grand Tour podium.
rider_signals.MÄDER Gino: positive: Part of decisive three-rider attack that gained 40 seconds forcing peloton abandon, tactical awareness in stage racing.
